title: Gregar Typho
content:
{{Top}} {{MultipleIssues|expand|image|updatecontent=[[Queen's Shadow]], [[Queen's Hope]], [[Star Wars: Timelines]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:CaptainTypho-2024ToppsSWHyperspace.png]] |name=Gregar Typho |homeworld=[[Naboo]]''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]'' |birth= |death=[[3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' Naboo''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy]]'' |species=[[Human]] {{C|[[Naboo (people)|Naboo]]}}{{Film|II}} |gender=[[Gender|Male]]{{Databank|captain-typho|Captain Typho}} |pronouns=He/him |height=1.85 [[meter]]s {{C|6 [[Foot|ft]], 1 in}}''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle!]]'' |mass= |hair=[[Color|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Brown''[[Queen's Shadow]]'' |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Royal House of Naboo]] **[[Royal Naboo Security Forces]] *[[Galactic Republic]] *[[Amidalans]][[Darth Vader (2020) 3|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 3]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We made it. I guess I was wrong. There was no danger at all.|Gregar Typho, to Padmé Amidala|audio=Typho-NoDanger-AOTC.flac|}} '''Gregar Typho''' was a [[human]] [[Gender|male]] [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]] and a [[captain]] of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces]] for [[Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala]] during the [[Separatist Crisis]] and the [[Clone Wars]], ensuring the [[senator]]'s safety at all costs. Typho followed in the footsteps of his uncle, [[Quarsh Panaka]], who was a Naboo Royal Security Forces captain during the [[Invasion of Naboo]] and his aunt, [[Mariek Panaka]], who also served as a captain in the Security Forces. In the last days of the [[Clone Wars]], Amidala decided to travel to the planet [[Mustafar]] to face [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]]. However, Typho tried to persuade her that he must go with her, to no avail, as Amidala informed him that [[C-3PO]] would look after her. By the [[time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War]], Typho had become part of the [[Amidalans]], a resistance group desired to hunt down and kill those responsible for Amidala's mysterious [[death]]. This eventually led to Typho's death at the hands of Darth Vader and his [[death trooper]]s. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Gregar Typho was the nephew of [[Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka]] and his [[Marriage|wife]] [[Mariek Panaka]]. His uncle served the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces]]. Typho had at least another relative, a cousin named [[Versé|Versaat]]. At some point, Gregar Typho undertook security forces training alongside [[Dormé|Dorra]], who stayed in the Academy to go through a modified version of [[Naboo Royal Handmaidens|handmaiden]] training. He was still a young man{{HelmetCollectionCite|58|Databank A-Z}} as of [[32 BBY]],{{FilmDate|I}} the year in which the [[Trade Federation]] [[megacorporation]] [[blockade]]d and [[Invasion of Naboo|invaded]] [[Naboo]]. During the invasion, Gregar was taken into custody by the Trade Federation and held at [[Camp Three (Naboo)|camp three]] alongside Naboo citizens who had been incarcerated within the campus dormitories of [[Gallo Mountain University]]. Typho went out of his way to protect his family whilst ensuring that food would be provided to the children within the camp. Upon finding a working transmitter, Typho assigned shifts to retain communications with the outside. While taking his turn during one of those shifts, he received word that the Queen of Naboo had arrived, opening the doorway for effective resistance to the Trade Federation. Gregar then began preparations for the oncoming [[Battle of Naboo|fight]].''[[Queen's Peril]]'' In the ensuing battle to retake the planet, he served with distinction{{HelmetCollectionCite|58|Databank A-Z}} and lost his left eye while fighting [[battle droid]]s. The battle ended in victory for the [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]], who allied with the [[Gungan]]s to secure victory while the [[Trade Federation Droid Army]] was shut down with the destruction of the ''[[Vuutun Palaa]]'' at the hands of the young [[Force-sensitive]] [[pilot]] [[Anakin Skywalker]]. After [[Padmé Amidala]]'s term as [[Monarch of Naboo|queen]] of Naboo ended and [[Gender|she]] accepted the role of [[Senator|representing]] Naboo in the [[Galactic Senate]], Typho immediately recommended Dorra as a potential new handmaiden. Typho succeeded his uncle as Amidala's head of security. ===Separatist Crisis=== {{Quote|I do hope he doesn't try anything foolish.''"
"''I'd be more concerned about ''her'' doing something than ''him''.|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Gregar Typho, on Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala|}} During the [[Separatist Crisis]], [[Captain]] Typho accompanied Amidala on a diplomatic mission to the [[The galaxy|galactic]] [[capital]] [[Coruscant]] to vote against the upcoming [[Military Creation Act]]. A growing [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist movement]] led by the charismatic former [[Jedi]] [[Count]] [[Dooku]] was challenging the ailing [[Galactic Republic]]. In response, [[Loyalist Committee|loyalist]] elements led by [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]] had introduced legislation to create a [[Grand Army of the Republic]]. [[File:CordeAndPadmeAssassinationAftermath.png|thumb|left|250px|Typho and Amidala survived the assassination attempt on the landing pad on Coruscant ]] Due to Amidala's high profile, the Senator and Captain Typho had traveled to Coruscant disguised as [[starfighter]] [[pilot]]s. Due to his grim duty, Typho enlisted [[Cordé]], one of Amidala's handmaidens, to pose as Amidala and travel on her [[J-type diplomatic barge|diplomatic barge]].{{ForceCollection|Gregar Typho|star=3}} This ruse enabled Amidala to escape an [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala|assassination attempt]] which claimed the life of her decoy and several members of her entourage. While Amidala was grieving over the fallen Cordé, Typho comforted her by assuring Amidala that the handmaiden had died in the line of duty. Later, Typho went to Amidala's apartment on Coruscant to establish security there. The Senator's security detail was further augmented by two [[Jedi]]: the [[Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker]], who had a crush on Amidala. That night, as Typho and his men guarded downstairs, a second assassination attempt against Amidala by the [[Clawdite]] assassin [[Zam Wesell]] involving two poisonous [[kouhun]]s was foiled by Skywalker and Kenobi. As a result, Amidala returned to Naboo accompanied by Skywalker. Following these events, Typho remained on Coruscant to protect the [[Gungan]] Representative [[Jar Jar Binks]], who took Amidala's place on the Senate. Binks sponsored the creation of the Grand Army, which foreshadowed the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars]]. ===Clone Wars=== ====New days for the Republic==== Several months into the war,{{EncyclopediaCite|Boba Fett and Other Characters of the Underworld}} Typho learned that several new battles had erupted across the [[Outer Rim Territories]]. He informed Amidala of the development, who deduced they were the reasoning behind several new security measures Palpatine had implemented on Naboo. Armed with that knowledge, Amidala made her way to Palpatine's office to discuss his security measures, only for the Chancellor to quickly reveal one of these new battles, the [[Battle of Teth]] in [[Wild Space]], had endangered Skywalker's life as he was blamed by the [[Hutt]] [[crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] for the kidnapping of his son, [[Rotta]], which changed Amidala's priorities.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] ====Blue Shadow Virus crisis==== {{Quote|Unless you want to become a box of spare parts, you'll tell us where that lab is!|Gregar Typho, to a captured tactical droid|{{TCW|Blue Shadow Virus (episode)}}}} After returning to Naboo at some point, Captain Typho and his men shot down and captured three [[B1-series battle droid]]s and a [[T-series military strategic analysis and tactics droid|T-series tactical droid]]. In response, the reigning [[Queen]] [[Neeyutnee]] summoned Senator Amidala and Representative Jar Jar Binks back to Naboo to request for military assistance from the Galactic Republic. Captain Typho was present when Amidala, Binks, and C-3PO interrogated the captured tactical droid. Tricking the droid into thinking that it was on a Separatist warship, C-3PO managed to glean that there was a secret Separatist research installation on Naboo, producing a virus. When the tactical droid realized that it was not on a Separatist warship, Typho raised a blaster at the droid and demanded that it cooperate. Before he could proceed, Binks accidentally destroyed the droid by knocking over some shelves while trying to catch a [[slug-beetle]]. Despite this setback, Amidala and Binks deduced that the droid came from Naboo's [[Eastern swamps]] due to the presence of mud on its chassis and the slug-beetle, which Binks recognized as originating from those swamps. While Amidala and company traveled to the Eastern Swamps to investigate, Captain Typho and Threepio remained behind to examine the other battle droids. While examining a B1 battle droid's head, Typho and C-3PO discovered a [[hologram]] of a Separatist [[scientist]] named [[Doctor]] [[Nuvo Vindi]] and quickly relayed this information to Amidala. Meanwhile, Amidala and her companions discovered [[Trade Federation laboratory|Dr. Vindi's laboratory]] in the Eastern Swamps. Vindi was developing a deadly bioweapon called the [[Blue Shadow Virus]] for use by the Separatists. Following a dangerous struggle, Amidala and her companions with the help of the Jedi Kenobi, Skywalker, his Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano]] and several [[clone trooper]]s managed to apprehend Vindi and stop him from releasing an airborne strain of the virus. Despite being exposed to the Blue Shadow Virus, Amidala and Binks survived due to Skywalker and Kenobi's efforts to obtain an antidote, the [[Reeksa|root of a reeksa plant]] from the planet [[Iego]].{{TCW|Mystery of a Thousand Moons}} ====Conference on Alderaan==== [[File:LikeLookingIntoABlueMirror-SoI.png|thumb|right|240px|Typho traveling with Amidala in the Senate Building]] Later in the Clone Wars, Typho accompanied Amidala to Alderaan for an [[Alderaan Refugee Conference]] hosted by Senator [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]] of Alderaan, which is to address the increasing humanitarian problem of the war refugees. However, after assassin [[Aurra Sing]]'s first attempt on Amidala, Typho began to closely watch and keep an eye on the assassin to prevent her from killing Amidala. He and Organa then went to her quarters to stop Sing, only to be unable to open the door. After they managed to get the door open, Typho picked Sing up and arrested her after she had been stunned by Amidala. They then took Sing back to Coruscant to be arrested.{{TCW|Assassin (episode)}} Shortly after, Amidala was back on Coruscant. Typho and his guards went with Amidala to show their support of Senator [[Riyo Chuchi]] after the Trade Federation blockaded her homeworld.{{TCW|Sphere of Influence}} ====Deregulation of the Banking Clan==== After a fight between [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] and Senator [[Rush Clovis]], Typho came into Amidala's room and asked what happened, to which Clovis tells him that they were attacked and Skywalker saved him. Typho then informed them that a [[2-1B surgical droid]] was on its way. His royal guards then helped carry Clovis to a bedroom.{{TCW|The Rise of Clovis}} ===Imperial Era=== ====Rise of the Empire==== {{Dialogue|Gregar Typho|My lady, let me come with you.|Padmé Amidala|There is no danger. The fighting is over, and… this is personal.|Gregar Typho|As you wish, My lady…but I strongly disagree.|Padmé Amidala|I'll be all right, Captain. This is something I must do myself. Besides, Threepio will look after me.|C-3PO|Oh, dear.|attr=Gregar Typho, Padmé Amidala, and C-3PO|src={{Film|III}}}} In the last days of the Clone Wars, the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] was [[Operation: Knightfall|attacked]] and many [[Jedi]] were [[Death|killed]] due to [[Order 66]]. Shortly after, Typho attended a meeting of the Senate where Palpatine declared the [[Proclamation of the New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire]] and himself as the [[Galactic Emperor]]. Almost immediately after, Amidala was visited by Kenobi, who told her impacting news about her husband. After Kenobi departed, Amidala decided to travel to the planet [[Mustafar]]. However, Typho tried to persuade her that he must go with her, to no avail, as Amidala informed him that [[C-3PO]] would look after her. Typho reluctantly abated, believing Threepio could offer her adequate protection. This turned out to be the last time Typho saw Amidala alive, as she died after [[Reproduction|giving birth]] to [[Luke Skywalker|her]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|children]] two days later on [[Polis Massa]]. ====Galactic Civil War==== Afterward, following Quarsh being appointed as the Imperial [[Moff]] of the [[Chommell sector]],''[[Leia, Princess of Alderaan]]'' Typho did not join him for his first formal address to the people of Naboo. Sabé noticed as such. Typho would instead join the [[Amidalans]] resistance cell to investigate Amidala's death. The group became convinced that Darth Vader had killed both Amidala and her Jedi protector, Anakin Skywalker, and set out to assassinate him. In [[3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of issues [[Darth Vader (2020) 3|three]], [[Darth Vader (2020) 4|four]], and [[Darth Vader (2020) 5|five]] of [[Star Wars: Darth Vader (2020)|''Star Wars: Darth Vader'' (2020)]] to [[3 ABY]]. Typho, alongside [[Tonra]] and Sabé, attempted to do so by luring Vader into a trap on Naboo, where the group led Vader into an underwater Gungan building, after which they used a [[beast call]]er to summon a [[Sando aqua monster]]. Typho and his group managed to force Vader out of the building, and into the creature's mouth, after which they released the beast caller, which set off away from the building. Vader was able to eventually escape and kill the beast, after which he set off to Theed, where he was ambushed by the Amidalans, killing most of them.[[Darth Vader (2020) 4|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 4]] Vader would again be attacked by the remaining Amidalans at Polis Massa, ultimately defeating the group.[[Darth Vader (2020) 5|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 5]] Typho died whilst fighting Vader and his [[death trooper]]s. ==Personality and traits== [[File:CaptainTypho-SWCT.png|thumb|right|250px|Typho was the loyal protector of Senator Amidala.]] Captain of the Royal Naboo Security Forces, Gregar Typho was absolutely loyal to Padmé Amidala, the senator of his homeworld. Typho deeply cared for Amidala, expressing his concern over her safety when Amidala had to return to Naboo to stop risking her life on Coruscant due the Separatists' attempt on her life. Unlike his uncle Quarsh Panaka, whom he succeeded in the security forces, Typho held the Jedi in high esteem, expressing his gratefulness to the Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi when Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker came to check Amidala on her apartment. Typho was a [[human]] male with black [[hair]], brown [[eye]]s and brown skin. After losing his left eye during the Battle of Naboo, he wore an eyepatch over the wound. He stood at 1.85 [[meter]]s (6 [[Foot|feet]], 1 inch) tall. ==Skills and Abiliites== As Padmé's head of security, Typho was highly proficient at armed combat, military tactics and security. His primary objective was protecting the senator from any potential threat or enemy. Padmé loved to partake in dangerous missions, in order to oppose any threat to Naboo and the Republic as a whole, which in turn made Typho's job more difficult. Typho worked in tandem with Padmé's handmaidens, to ensure her safety. He was also a skilled starfighter pilot, and flew a Naboo [[N-1 starfighter|N-1 Starfighter]], when he arrived on Coruscant alongside Padmé, as they escorted Padmé's [[J-type diplomatic barge]] to the Military Creation Act. ==Behind the scenes== Gregar Typho was portrayed by [[Jay Laga'aia]] in both the [[2002]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] and the [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. He was voiced by [[James Mathis III]] in ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' TV series, which aired from [[2008]] to [[2013]]. In early storyboards of ''Attack of the Clones'', Typho was originally intended to be gunned down by [[droideka]]s upon waving goodbye to Amidala. This idea, however, was dropped.''[[Star Wars Storyboards: The Prequel Trilogy]]'' In [[E. K. Johnston]]'s [[2019]] novel ''[[Queen's Shadow]]'', Gregar Typho's first name is misspelled as "Gregor." This misspelling was repeated in ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy]]'' released by [[Dorling Kindersley|DK]] on [[November 19]], [[2024]].''[[Star Wars
